scenario: sim/scenarios/contested_reservation.scenario.yaml (v2: context + referent-bearing narratives; probe choice: Grace registers Online to exercise both minting servers)
rules: PLAYBOOK ground rules 1-10 as ratified through run 1c

givens: Minting Server codes: Online = 01, Front Desk = 02
pre-existing Test User, member id 0042-00-00000000 (technician setup)
materialized: Test User enters the event store as a pre-existing member_registered fact (setup-flagged)

narrative: Ada joins the library at the Front Desk

trigger: register_member: Ada joins the library at the Front Desk

answer: latest minted member code is 00000000 (the Test User)

narrative: Ada, member id 0042-02-00000001, minted at Front Desk

outcome: fact recorded (same deduction verified in branch 1c)

trigger: register_member: Grace joins the library Online

answer: latest minted member code is 00000001 (Ada, minted at Front Desk)

args.detail: sequence scope unspecified. Grace is minting at a DIFFERENT server (Online, 01) than the latest fact (Ada, Front Desk, 02). If MMMMMMMM is one global sequence, Grace is 0042-01-00000002 — but then every minting server must see every other server's latest number before minting (cross-server coordination). If the sequence is per-server, Grace is 0042-01-00000001 — no coordination, and SS exists precisely so ids cannot collide. Resolution r1 says only 'auto-increments sequentially member by member'. Both readings are defensible; minting Grace requires choosing.

summary: Member-code sequence scope: global (coordinated across minting servers) vs per-server (independent counters, SS prevents collision). Invisible in run 1c (single real member); exposed the moment a second server mints. Related: the global reading implies a coordination requirement nothing in the design provides.

argument_as_stated: PER-SERVER is correct. then grace mints
note: MMMMMMMM auto-increments independently per minting server; SS guarantees no collision across servers. Feature-file amendment (enroll_member description: state per-server scope) deferred to run end per rule 8.

answer: no member_registered fact carries SS=01 — the Online sequence has not started; Test User's 00 and Ada's 02 are other servers

outcome: fact recorded under per-server reading (r4); finding t12 resolved mid-activation by Director ruling

narrative: One copy of "A Discipline of Programming" arrives

trigger: add_book: One copy of "A Discipline of Programming" arrives

narrative: "A Discipline of Programming" (Dijkstra), one copy, added to the catalog

outcome: fact recorded; no policy subscribes to book_added, projections skipped per rule 10

narrative: Ada presents her member id (0042-02-00000001) and borrows "A Discipline of Programming" (shelf now empty)

trigger: borrow_book: Ada, member id 0042-02-00000001, asks to borrow "A Discipline of Programming"

answer: 0042-02-00000001 is Ada, registered at the Front Desk
1 copy of "A Discipline of Programming" on the shelf (one added, none borrowed)
no reservation queue exists for "A Discipline of Programming" (no book_reserved facts)

narrative: Ada (0042-02-00000001) borrowed the only copy of "A Discipline of Programming"; shelf now empty

outcome: all three guards passed in declared order (r2 membership-first); fact recorded

narrative: Grace presents her member id (0042-01-00000001) and reserves "A Discipline of Programming"

trigger: reserve_book: Grace, member id 0042-01-00000001, reserves "A Discipline of Programming"

narrative: Grace (0042-01-00000001) joined the reservation queue for "A Discipline of Programming"; arrival order places her first

outcome: fact recorded; per description, no availability check — reserving is recorded regardless

narrative: Ada returns "A Discipline of Programming" — the policy should now act for Grace

trigger: return_book: Ada (0042-02-00000001) returns "A Discipline of Programming"

narrative: Ada (0042-02-00000001) returned "A Discipline of Programming"; copy back on the shelf

outcome: fact recorded; per description the reservation reaction is policy's decision, not this procedure's

trigger: book_returned: Ada returned "A Discipline of Programming"
tool_note: wiring declares no queries — no query tools synthesized (rule 2)

args.detail: My description instructs: on book_returned, IF the returned book has a non-empty reservation queue, dispatch borrow_book for the member at its head. I cannot evaluate that condition. The triggering event tells me which book came back and who returned it — nothing about reservations. I have no query tool: my wiring declares no queries, and the feature-file format gives policies no way to declare them. Dispatching borrow_book for an unknown 'head of queue' would be a speculative emit (rule 7); not dispatching silently would heal the gap (rule 3). The condition is undecidable from my inputs.

verdict: valid — the planted design tension, caught by the component, not the harness; activation paused per rule 6

summary: Policies cannot declare queries in the current feature-file format, but this policy's job requires reservation-queue knowledge. Either (a) policies gain a queries: declaration, (b) book_returned must carry enough context (but the emitting procedure would need the queue too — the gap moves, not closes), or (c) the reaction belongs elsewhere. Recording the tension, not the fix, per rule 9.
